mission.title: opentofu: Allow arbitrary expressions in the `prevent_destroy` argument of a `resource` lifecycle block
mission.description: Allow arbitrary expressions in the `prevent_destroy` argument of a `resource` lifecycle block. Requested by 58+ users.

steps.title: Check OpenTofu installation
steps.description:
Verify your OpenTofu version:
```bash
tofu version
```
This feature requires OpenTofu installed locally. Install via `brew install opentofu` or download from https://opentofu.org/docs/intro/install/.

steps.title: Understand the feature context
steps.description: This is a sub-issue of https://github.com/opentofu/opentofu/issues/1329, discussing details specific to the `prevent_destroy` lifecycle argument.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Allow arbitrary expressions in the `prevent_destroy`…
steps.description:
This is a prototype for #2522.

Previously we always evaluated prevent_destroy for a managed resource at config load time, requiring it to be static.

However, we don't actually need the value until runtime so we can instead defer evaluation of the expression and populate it with a dynamic
```hcl
╷
│ Error: Variables not allowed
│ 
│   on prevent-destroy-dynamic.tf line 7, in resource "aws_db_instance" "example":
│    7:     prevent_destroy = var.prevent_database_destroy
│ 
│ Variables may not be used here.
╵
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the feature is working as expected:
```bash
tofu version
tofu validate
tofu plan
```
Confirm that the feature described in "Allow arbitrary expressions in the `prevent_destroy` argument of a `resource` lifecycle block" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This is a prototype for #2522.

Previously we always evaluated prevent_destroy for a managed resource at config load time, requiring it to be static.

However, we don't actually need the value until runtime so we can instead defer evaluation of the expression and populate it with a dynamic expression result, allowing modules to populate it based on input variables and other external data.

prerequisites.description: OpenTofu CLI installed (brew install opentofu or download from opentofu.org). Terraform provider credentials as needed for the specific configuration.
